government_contract_award: New federal funding means new parts to build — and often a scramble for qualified machining capacity BTX can fill.
contract_win: A production win expands their build rate; sub-tier and overflow machining demand usually follows.
contract_loss: A lost program can free them to re-source work — an opening for a new supplier like BTX.
demand_spike: Orders are outrunning capacity — a classic window to win overflow or second-source work.
hiring_surge: Headcount growth signals a ramp; engineering and supply-chain buyers are active now.
capacity_constraint: Their floor is full — outsourcing precision work to BTX relieves the bottleneck.
supplier_delay: A shaky supplier puts their schedule at risk; BTX can pitch as a reliable second source.
quality_escape: A quality miss invites a supplier review BTX can compete in on AS9100 rigor.
pricing_pressure: Cost inflation makes them shop suppliers — a price/value opening.
regulatory_change: New compliance requirements favor certified shops; BTX's AS9100/ITAR status is a wedge.
competitor_expansion: A rival is adding capacity — competitive pressure to track, not a direct sale.
competitor_won_deal: A competitor took a deal in play; understand why before the next pursuit.
